audit_status.source_base: Greek 2 Corinthians text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for painful visit/letter, forgiveness/restoration of offender, adversarial advantage, Troas, fragrance of Messiah, sincerity.

verses.restored: For I judged this for myself: not to come again to you in grief.
For if I grieve you, who then makes me glad except the one being grieved by me?
And I wrote this very thing, so that when I came I might not have grief from those from whom it was necessary for me to rejoice, having confidence in all of you that my joy is the joy of you all.
For out of much pressure and compression of heart I wrote to you through many tears, not so that you might be grieved, but so that you might know the love I have more abundantly for you.
But if anyone has caused grief, that one has not grieved me, but in part, so that I may not weigh down, all of you.
Sufficient for such a one is this rebuke by the majority,
so that instead you should rather graciously forgive and comfort/encourage, lest such a one be swallowed by more abundant grief.
Therefore I urge you to confirm love toward such a one.
For into this also I wrote, so that I might know your tested character, whether you are obedient in all things.
To whom you graciously forgive anything, I also; for indeed what I have graciously forgiven, if I have forgiven anything, it is because of you in the face/presence of Messiah,
so that we may not be outwitted by the adversarial one, for we are not unaware of its thoughts.
Coming to Troas for the good news of Messiah, and a door having been opened to me in the Lord,
I had no rest in my spirit because I did not find Titus my sibling; but saying farewell to them, I went out to Macedonia.
But thanks to the Divine, the One always leading us in triumphal procession in Messiah and making manifest through us the fragrance of his knowledge in every place.
For we are aroma of Messiah to the Divine among those being saved/healed and among those perishing:
to one, fragrance from death into death; to the other, fragrance from life into life. And who is sufficient for these things?
For we are not like the many, peddling the word of the Divine, but as from sincerity, but as from the Divine, before the Divine, we speak in Messiah.

translation_notes: Forgiveness/restoration in verses 6-8 is essential: discipline must not swallow the person in grief.
Fragrance/triumph imagery is rhetorically complex; preserve both the vulnerable apostolic role and the manifesting of Messiah's knowledge.
